reviews.review_msg: bro was lowk confused all term and ykw if he can become a prof and get paid thousands a year, i think i can do that too.
*I never wore a seatbelt omw to his class* that's how bad this class is. Take Michael Hamilton OR ANYBODY ELSE. Not Rebecca goyan tho. For all the future kids please listen to this.

Loren was alright. I would assume it was his first time teaching Chem 122, and, to be expected, it wasn't taught well. Little info on slides for students to understand. Exams are way harder than anticipated by the class; the averages were around 50% for both exams. I would avoid taking it with him, as it was definitely a struggle.(scaled the class)

Nice guy. Teaching clearly isn't his strong suit and he doesn't seem to be interested in this class, hence the bad reviews. He is a nice and caring professor. I agree he's not the best but most complaints are exaggerated by people who can't take matters into their own hands.

Was pretty bad at answering questions, often not even properly answering them at all. Gave 5 practice questions on the midterm (all were easy) then having the midterm itself be much more difficult. Says there's "no trick questions" on exams then admits he didn't even make it. Also released the final grade before releasing results for the final exam

Nice professor, not the best explainer but rating is too harsh. It is possible to do well if you do textbook questions and any extra questions provided. Lecture is more helpful if you preview the unit beforehand, because he often covers problem-solving.

Seems like a nice guy, but he cannot explain if his life depended on it! Chem is not my strong suit, but the topics were easy enough to understand on your own. I would not take his class unless it is necessary. Get ready to read the textbook and watch a lot of videos if you want a good mark.
Just dont take Chem with him

Not the greatest teacher, but the course itself isn't that difficult. Doesn't always give the best explanations of topics so a lot of at home review is needed. Doesn't give a clear criteria for midterms, formula sheets are also quite messy. Refer to the textbook and use external sources as well for help because office hours are really hit or miss
You're going to have to know the textbook inside out like it's the ABCs.

Nothing will be achieved by going to lectures. Nice guy and seems very compassionate. He can not clearly get through his lecture slides and the students need correct him when he works through example questions. He is not a confident speaker and sentences start loud and trail off into muttering.

Professor is incredibly kind and supportive, always taking the time to make sure everyone understands the material. They explain complex concepts clearly and patiently, creating a comfortable environment where questions are encouraged. Would definitely take again.
Worst ever

AVOID THIS PROF. When questions were asked he would say "that's a good question" and admit to not knowing how to answer it. It seemed like he was learning the material with the class. Vague on what was going to be on exams and gave 6 practice problems that were beginner level and made the midterm a lot harder. Final practice didn't reflect on final
The lecture and practice questions are way different and in another level in compare to exams.
Avoid this prof. Hopefully he changes his teaching stylle for his next classes.
Very bad lecturer, avoid at all costs.

He is a nice guy and sometimes funny, but he is so confusing, doesn't go over whats going to be on the midterm, and doesn't explain any of the questions. so many of practice questions are clearly ai.easier just to learn the course your self and pray for the exams.

Professor Kaake is nice, but his teaching style has me confused.. Often times it seemed like he was learning the materiel with us and instead of doing practice questions he would derive equations (despite this not being testable). That said, the experiments on day 1 were pretty fun, and he is nice and patient if you go see him in office hours.
"any questions?"
genuinely cannot teach at all. i feel like he's more confused than we are most of the time. so unhelpful and doesn't know what he's doing. him and megan barker would be best friends.

He started confusing students from day one, with not going over the syllabus. His teaching is extremely confusing and it is hard to learn anything in the lecture or from his slides, so you need to do outside learning. He seems confused with the lectures himself and his exam DID NOT reflect what he gave for practice and Homework's. Worst prof ever.

please stay away from this prof, please. id rather I fall behind a semester than ever be in the class again. He's not very helpful, he said no trick question on the midterm.... guess he lied. Doesn't even teach you how to do the questions in lecture btw.
Wouldn't answer questions properly, reads mainly off the slides with little to no practice questions being done.
Doesn't do good with answering questions. Midterm was nothing like the things he teaches in class or tutorial.

Worst prof I have had so far. Released minimal questions for exam prep and was not made by prof. Exam was incredibly difficult with most questions not really reflecting what we learnt. Lectures are not helpful. avoids questions and very poor at explaining topics. not showing or allowing any discussion on the midterm and is not scaling much.

For the second time this semester, he failed to upload notes and didn't fix it before class. He gave just six easy practice questions for a much harder midterm (class avg 50%), shared the formula sheet four days prior, won't release exams, and reuses old tests. Frequent mistakes, dismissive answers, and unhelpful office hours. Wish I could say more

He said earlier this school,that his exam questions were in fact so simple that we would be surprised if they weren't trick questions. As of now mid way through the course after completing our midterm, it turns out he didn't even make the exam himself and now backtracked saying that the questions are meant to trick you and poke out common mistakes.

He's so confusing. Every time someone asks a question in class, he talks for a bit and then admits he doesn't know the answer. Also very unclear with Loncapa deadlines, etc. He gave us 6 practice MCQs for the midterm and admitted he had no idea what was even on said midterm (1/3 of grade). The midterm class average was really low.

Bro doesn't write his own exams, he steals them from the other profs. Anytime you ask him a question, he says he doesn't know and tells you to come to his office hours. The practice midterm he gave had 6 questions and was not like the actual midterm at all. I took Garry for granted for Chem 121 and I miss him 💔

Just keep in mind, going into this course he will "NOT" give you any hints on potential type questions for the exams. Not a clear grading criteria. Likes to show off that he knows how to derive equations by writing them on 5 whiteboards, but not a single one in exam. The Assignments are hard, requires a lot of work above and beyond his lecture.

The class overall is not very difficult. The homework questions require some thinking, but if you refer to the lectures notes while doing them, they are manageable. There are also extra credit after every homework. IMO doing the homework also is essential for doing well in the exams. In brief: DO THE HOMEWORK, and youll enjoy this class a lot.
He seems very enthusiastic about the material but lectures and exam were not connected at all, better off reading and understanding all concepts from the book.

Lectures are irrelevant for the most part the key to them would be the last few slides that say learning objectives, study those. Read the textbook, it's absolutely necessary because the lecture notes aren't enough. Overall nice prof

Very nice teacher who seems almost overly polite and wants nothing more to share his knowledge with students.  Powerpoints were good although sometimes would focus too much on derivation understanding of the concepts was less stressed.  Tests had very little math and mainly consisted of ranking things in different orders.  Overall I would recommend
